message:
🌱 Add kserve: Protect KFServing endpoint mission (#1661)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,83 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kserve-760-protect-kfserving-endpoint",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kserve: Protect KFServing endpoint",
+    "description": "Protect KFServing endpoint. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kserve deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kserve version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
helm list -n kserve 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kserve installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kserve configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kserve configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get configmap -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kserve
```
Currently, a `kfserving-gateway` is used for kfserving inference endpoints. It uses LoadBalancer and all endpoints are publicly available without protection. 

This doesn't meet requirement for production grade cluster."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Protect KFServing endpoint",
+        "description": "The story is a bit complicated but I think there is no behavior change as far as I know for Istio/Dex. I do not think KFServing ever worked in Kubeflow with Auth due to various issues we found along the way.

- KFServing was initially shipped in Kubeflow 0.7 with KFServing 0.2.0(which is later
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: kfserving-kubeflow-yuzi-dan-kfserving-test
  namespace: kubeflow-yuzi-dan
spec:
  gateways:
  - kubeflow/kubeflow-gateway
  hosts:
  - '*'
  http:
  - match:
    - uri:
        prefix: /kfserving/kubeflow-yuzi-dan/sklearn-iris
    rewrite:
        uri: /v1/models/sklearn-iris:predict
    route:
    - destination:
        host: cluster-local-gateway.istio-system.svc.cluster.local
      headers:
        request:
          set:
            Host: sklearn-iris-predictor-default.kubeflow-yuzi-dan.svc.cluster.local
      
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade kserve to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kserve:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kserve kserve/kserve --namespace kserve
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve
helm list -n kserve
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get events -n kserve --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Protect KFServing endpoint" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The story is a bit complicated but I think there is no behavior change as far as I know for Istio/Dex. I do not think KFServing ever worked in Kubeflow with Auth due to various issues we found along the way.

- KFServing was initially shipped in Kubeflow 0.7 with KFServing 0.2.0(which is later patched with 0.2.2 for pod mutator issue in 0.7.1)  and Knative 0.8.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: kfserving-kubeflow-yuzi-dan-kfserving-test
  namespace: kubeflow-yuzi-dan
spec:
  gateways:
  - kubeflow/kubeflow-gateway
  hosts:
  - '*'
  http:
  - match:
    - uri:
        prefix: /kfserving/kubeflow-yuzi-dan/sklearn-iris
    rewrite:
        uri: /v1/models/sklearn-iris:predict
    route:
    - destination:
        host: cluster-local-gateway.istio-system.svc.cluster.local
      headers:
        request:
          set:
            Host: sklearn-iris-predictor-default.kubeflow-yuzi-dan.svc.cluster.local
      weight: 100
    timeout: 300s",
+        "python iap_request.py https://kfserving.endpoints.kfserving-248310.cloud.goog/
kfserving/kubeflow-yuzi-dan/sklearn-iris 
165686043417-vq3k1nd60m4svttrj8f1r749augem3fl.apps.googleusercontent.com --input=./docs/samples/sklearn/iris-input.json

{"predictions": [1, 1]}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kserve",
+      "incubating",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kserve"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Ingress",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "incubating",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/kserve/kserve/issues/760",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kserve/kserve"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 29,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kserve installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-22T06:24:01.157Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
